blocks.text: This sample demonstrates how pdfnative-cli handles multi-font documents. Each font entry maps a language tag to a registered TTF loader. Latin text falls back to the built-in Helvetica when no explicit font entry is present.

Register a CJK font loader before rendering: registerFonts({ ja: () => import('pdfnative/fonts/noto-jp-data.js') }). Then await loadFontData('ja') and pass the result in fontEntries when calling buildDocumentPDFBytes. See 04-multilingual.js for a complete working example using four scripts simultaneously.

items: Latin (A-Z, diacritics): built-in Helvetica / Bold — zero configuration
CJK (Japanese, Chinese, Korean): requires a registered Noto Sans CJK TTF loader
Script detection is per-character — a single paragraph may use multiple fonts
Font subsetting: only referenced glyphs are embedded, keeping file size small

metadata.subject: Multilingual font support — CJK loader registration pattern
metadata.keywords: multilang, japanese, cjk, noto, fonts, loader
